workflows.name: Patient Registration & Demographics
source_systems: bahmni-core (CSV Data Import, OpenELIS Patient Feed Sync)
workflows.local_equivalent: patient-core-service (MRN+party FK, no-PHI state machine, outbox) + party-core-service + healthstack-patient-service (PHI overlay DOB/SSN-AES256GCM, cross-schema FK, merged view) + healthstack-consent-service
local_gaps: healthstack-patient FK currently in-memory pending M9 Drizzle (data-truth rule violation)
no self-serve person-side pre-registration intake form wired (builder-core exists but no registration form template)
bulk/feed import (CSV, OpenELIS-style atom feed) not exposed as a workflow-core process

suggested_representation.trigger: person self-starts pre-registration (patient-app) OR front-desk creates record OR inbound feed/CSV import event
suggested_representation.permissions: RBAC registrar role for create; person owns own demographic edits via patient-app; ABAC tenant isolation; ConsentGuard on PHI overlay read/write
notifications: welcome + portal-invite to person (notify-service multi-channel)
duplicate-found alert to registrar
suggested_representation.audit: audit-core SHA-256 hash-chain on every demographic write + GDPR Art.15 disclosure
failure_modes: feed conflict (needs human conflict resolution per OpenELIS sync)

workflows.person_centric_reshape: Source flow is registrar-form-first (Bahmni/VistA admin entry). Re-center so the person starts their own profile from the patient-app (pre-fill demographics, upload ID, consent choices) before ever reaching a desk; the registrar role becomes verify+merge, not data-entry. The org's MPI/dedup requirement is satisfied as a consequence of giving the person one durable owned identity.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native workflow-core (BPMN registration process) + patient-core/party-core/healthstack-patient services; feed/CSV import as integrations-core connector + source background service pattern

workflows.name: Appointment Booking & Check-in
source_files: scheduling FHIR Appointment/Slot patterns from source corpus
workflows.local_equivalent: scheduling-service (FHIR Appointment/Slot, desk metrics/alerts, double-booking prevention) + calendar-core-service (RRULE) + business-scheduling-service (scaffold) + notify-service
local_gaps: no person-facing self-book + check-in journey wired (patient-app)
reminder/recall automation not configured on automation-core

suggested_representation.trigger: person requests slot (self-book) OR provider/desk books OR recall-due automation fires
suggested_representation.permissions: person books own appointments within open slots; desk role overrides; ABAC by site/department; ConsentGuard on any PHI shown
ui: patient-app self-book + check-in (QR/geofence arrival via personal-tracking)
notifications: multi-channel reminders + waitlist-open offers + check-in confirmation (notify-service SSE)

workflows.person_centric_reshape: Replace the desk-driven schedule grid as primary with a person-first journey: the person sees real open slots, self-books, gets reminders, and checks in from their phone (geofence arrival auto-check-in via personal-tracking). The desk's capacity/double-booking/queue-management requirements ride on top of the same Appointment/Slot data. Recall becomes a kindness ('you're due') not an org backlog.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native workflow-core (check-in/queue process) + scheduling-core/calendar-core services + automation-core (reminders/recall cron); finish business-scheduling-service overlay

workflows.name: Triage / Risk-flag Evaluation
workflows.local_equivalent: workflow-core-service (rules/incidents) + automation-core-service (conditional branching) + terminology-service + clinical-decision-support is NOT yet a service (gap) + tasks-core for resulting actions
local_gaps: no dedicated rules/CDS engine service (asrcm-style risk-model admin + evaluation)
reminder-due rule evaluation (vista-dashboard-rules forecast) not modeled
triage scoring (queue acuity) absent in scheduling/encounter

suggested_representation.trigger: encounter created / vitals captured / scheduled rule-evaluation cron / inbound result event
suggested_representation.permissions: clinical roles evaluate; CDS-admin authors rules; ConsentGuard on PHI inputs; break-glass logged
ui: clinician-app triage board with acuity color-coding (vista-dashboard pattern)
notifications: flag-to-clinician (notify)
escalation if unacknowledged (links to critical-alert workflow)
suggested_representation.audit: audit-core on flag raise/ack/clear; rule version recorded with each evaluation

workflows.person_centric_reshape: Source dashboards are org/team-centric ('notes due by team', provider worklists). Re-center: each person carries their own risk/care-gap timeline; the clinician's team-worklist is a roll-up view of person timelines. Person sees plain-language 'you're due for X' and 'your result needs follow-up', turning org compliance reminders into the person's own health agenda.
workflows.implementation_mode: custom state machine for risk scoring (asrcm-style) wrapped as a CDS rules service + local native workflow-core for evaluation orchestration + automation-core scheduled re-evaluation

workflows.name: Encounter Documentation
workflows.local_equivalent: encounter-service (FHIR Encounter contract + auth probes, clinical models NOT built - partial) + clinical-doc-service (FHIR Composition/DocumentReference, signatures) + healthstack-notes-service (scaffold) + orders-service
local_gaps: encounter-service clinical models not yet built (partial)

suggested_representation.trigger: check-in completes -> encounter opened; provider opens chart
suggested_representation.permissions: author write; co-sign role; ConsentGuard on every clinical read/write; amendment requires reason
notifications: co-sign request
AVS ready to person
suggested_representation.audit: audit-core + clinical-doc signature chain; amendments tamper-evident
failure_modes: partial save (encounter transaction must be atomic)

workflows.person_centric_reshape: Bahmni's encounter-transaction is a clinician save bundle. Re-center the output toward the person: every signed encounter immediately yields a plain-language after-visit summary the person owns, and the person can read (not edit) their problems/orders/notes in patient-app. Clinician documentation completeness/billing-capture requirements are satisfied as the byproduct of producing that person-readable record.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native workflow-core (encounter lifecycle) + build out encounter-service clinical models + clinical-doc-service; finish healthstack-notes/problems services

workflows.name: Lab Order + Result Review
source_systems: vista-fhir-codex (Lab Result Extraction with Panel Grouping)
workflows.local_equivalent: orders-service (FHIR ServiceRequest CPOE, Observation/DiagnosticReport results, attestation) + healthstack-lab-service (scaffold-only) + terminology-service (LOINC)
local_gaps: no LIS/OpenELIS atom-feed inbound result connector
critical-value detection not wired to alert workflow

suggested_representation.trigger: provider places lab order (CPOE) -> specimen collected -> instrument/LIS result inbound
suggested_representation.permissions: order role; result-review attestation; ConsentGuard; critical results escalate regardless
ui: clinician-app result inbox with panel grouping + trend
patient-app results with plain-language interpretation + 'what to do'
notifications: result-ready to provider + person
critical -> escalation path (hc.critical_alert)

workflows.person_centric_reshape: VistA routes results to a provider inbox; the person waits. Re-center so the person sees their result the moment it is reviewed-safe, with a plain-language interpretation, reference ranges, and trend, plus a clear next-step. The provider's review/attestation requirement and the lab's accession/panel rigor are preserved but become the gate that makes the person-facing result trustworthy, not a wall the person sits behind.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native workflow-core (order->result->review) + orders-service + build healthstack-lab-service; LIS inbound as integrations-core connector + source background service (atom-feed) pattern; critical-value scanner feeds hc.critical_alert

workflows.name: Radiology Order + Result Review (Protocol/Contraindication)
source_systems: RAPTOR (Order Intake -> Protocol Selection -> Scheduling; Contraindication Evaluation)
workflows.local_equivalent: orders-service (ServiceRequest) + healthstack-imaging-service (scaffold-only, FHIR ImagingStudy/DICOM/PACS TBD) + workflow-core (protocol selection states) + terminology
local_gaps: no protocol library / contraindication rules engine (RAPTOR ContraIndEngine analog)
abnormal-imaging alert not wired to critical-alert workflow

suggested_representation.trigger: provider orders imaging study
suggested_representation.permissions: order role; radiologist protocol/read; ConsentGuard; contraindication override requires reason
notifications: protocol prep instructions to person
abnormal -> escalation
suggested_representation.audit: audit-core on protocol/override/read; contraindication decision logged

workflows.person_centric_reshape: RAPTOR is radiologist-workflow-centric (worklist, protocol, schedule). Re-center: the person gets prep instructions, contraindication safety checks done before they arrive (allergies/meds pulled automatically), and a plain-language result with next steps. Radiologist protocol/contraindication rigor becomes the safety wrapper around the person's study, not an internal-only worklist.
workflows.implementation_mode: custom state machine for protocol/contraindication (RAPTOR ContraIndEngine analog) as a rules module + local native workflow-core orchestration + build healthstack-imaging-service; PACS via integrations-core; abnormal feeds hc.critical_alert

workflows.name: Prescription / Medication Order
workflows.local_equivalent: orders-service (CPOE) + healthstack-meds-service (scaffold-only, FHIR Medication TBD) + terminology (RxNorm) + healthstack-consent
local_gaps: no pharmacy review queue / MAR (administration record)

suggested_representation.trigger: provider prescribes medication
suggested_representation.permissions: prescriber role; pharmacist verify; nurse MAR at bedside; ConsentGuard; controlled-substance extra auth
ui: prescriber order entry w/ interaction alerts
patient-app med list + refill request + instructions
notifications: interaction alert to prescriber
refill-ready to person
verify request to pharmacist
suggested_representation.audit: audit-core full med chain; controlled-substance tamper-evident

workflows.person_centric_reshape: VistA medication admin is inpatient-pharmacy-process-centric. Re-center on the person's medication life: one current med list they own, plain-language instructions, self-serve refill requests, adherence reminders, and 'why am I on this'. Pharmacist verification and drug-interaction safety (GMRA-style) are preserved as the safety layer that protects the person, and the inpatient MAR rigor still satisfies administration compliance.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native workflow-core (prescribe->verify->administer) + orders-service + build healthstack-meds-service + interaction rules module; e-prescribe + refill via integrations-core/automation-core

workflows.name: Referral Management
source_systems: aware (Urology Consult order dialog on alert ack)
source_files: orders ServiceRequest referral patterns from corpus
workflows.local_equivalent: orders-service (ServiceRequest referral) + tasks-core (assignment/dependencies) + workflow-core (referral lifecycle) + site-core (provider roster) + notify-service
local_gaps: no provider-directory match for referral target
closed-loop referral (report back to referrer) not modeled

suggested_representation.trigger: provider creates referral (ServiceRequest) or alert-ack triggers consult order
suggested_representation.permissions: provider create; receiving accept; coordinator route; ConsentGuard on shared clinical data
notifications: referral received/accepted/scheduled to person + referrer
report-back ready
suggested_representation.audit: audit-core on every state; consent for cross-org share

workflows.person_centric_reshape: Referrals in source systems are provider-to-provider order objects; the person often falls through the gap. Re-center on a person-owned referral journey: the person sees the referral, the chosen specialist, the appointment, and gets nudged through each step, with the loop only 'closed' when the report comes back to their record. The org's leakage/closed-loop-compliance metric is satisfied because the person was carried all the way through.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native workflow-core (closed-loop referral process) + orders-service + tasks-core + site-core provider directory; external referral exchange via integrations-core

workflows.name: Claim / Healthcare Billing & Revenue Cycle
workflows.local_equivalent: healthstack-billing-service (claims worklist, eligibility/submit, copay/payment, IN-MEMORY store #737) + healthstack-claims-service (scaffold, overlaps billing) + accounting-core-service (double-entry ledger, partial)
local_gaps: healthstack-claims-service scaffold-only and overlaps billing (boundary unclear)

suggested_representation.trigger: encounter/service completes -> charge capture; periodic claim batch
suggested_representation.permissions: biller role; manager for adjustments/write-offs; person pays own balance; ABAC tenant
notifications: claim status + balance to person
denial to biller

workflows.person_centric_reshape: VistA/ERP billing is back-office revenue-cycle-centric. Re-center on financial clarity for the person: up-front cost estimates, a single transparent statement in plain language, and easy self-pay/plans. The org's full claim/coding/denial/AR machinery is preserved (no feature loss) but runs behind a person-facing surface that removes billing surprise, which simplifies collections too.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native workflow-core (revenue-cycle process) + healthstack-billing-service (must move off in-memory to Postgres) + accounting-core ledger; resolve billing/claims service boundary; payer integration via integrations-core

workflows.name: Critical Result Alert & Follow-up Escalation
source_files: aware AlertCache.xml, AlertIntercept.pas, SQLTransporter.cs, AwareQIManager Default.aspx.cs
workflows.local_equivalent: notify-service (multi-channel, SSE, delivery tracking) + tasks-core (follow-up tasks) + workflow-core (escalation states) + audit-core
local_gaps: no alert-cache / unacknowledged-alert intercept on chart open (AWARE DLL analog)
no time-based escalation ladder (>7 days no action -> supervisor)
no QI compliance reporting on alert follow-up

suggested_representation.trigger: critical lab/imaging result detected (abnormal CXR/mammogram/PSA/FOBT-FIT etc.)
suggested_representation.permissions: provider ack/act; supervisor on escalation; QI read compliance; ConsentGuard; alert never suppressed
jobs: daily QI ETL/compliance calc (AWARE SQL Transporter analog)
notifications: multi-channel to provider; person notified result needs attention; supervisor on escalation
suggested_representation.audit: audit-core full ack/escalation chain; tamper-evident; QI metrics derived from audit
failure_modes: person not informed of own critical result

workflows.person_centric_reshape: AWARE is a provider-acknowledgement + QI-supervisor system; the person is invisible. Re-center to guarantee the person is never the last to know: the person is informed their result needs follow-up and can see the action plan, while the provider-ack + time-based escalation + supervisor + QI-compliance machinery (the whole AWARE value) is preserved as the safety net that ensures someone always acts. No feature lost; a person-protection layer added.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native workflow-core (escalation ladder state machine) + notify-service + tasks-core + audit-core-derived QI; chart-open intercept as a UI guard; result events from hc.lab/hc.radiology

workflows.name: After-Visit Summary (AVS) Generation
source_systems: avs (AVS Generation, Print/Export, Content Customization, Admin Config, Health Education Integration)
workflows.local_equivalent: reports-service (Gotenberg PDF, scheduled jobs) + clinical-doc-service (Composition) + documents-core + healthstack-education-service (scaffold) + notify-service
local_gaps: no AVS assembler aggregating demographics/vitals/labs/meds/allergies/appointments into one person document

suggested_representation.trigger: encounter signed/closed -> auto-generate AVS; provider opens AVS wizard
suggested_representation.permissions: provider customize; person owns delivered AVS; admin configures sections/translations; ConsentGuard on PHI
ui: provider AVS wizard + content/comments editor (TinyMCE analog)
jobs: parallel data-collection on generate (avs thread-fan-out pattern)
notifications: AVS ready to person (notify multi-channel)

workflows.person_centric_reshape: AVS is already person-output but generated through a provider/admin wizard. Re-center fully: the person automatically receives a clear, translated, education-linked summary in patient-app the moment the visit closes (no provider step required for the baseline), with provider customization optional. The facility's section/disclaimer/translation governance is preserved as defaults, satisfying compliance while the person gets a genuinely useful takeaway.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native workflow-core (post-encounter AVS process) + reports-service (Gotenberg PDF) + clinical-doc + documents-core; parallel aggregation via automation-core; education via integrations-core; build healthstack-education-service

workflows.name: Maternity / Pregnancy Tracking
source_systems: maternity-tracker (8 workflows: flag eval, DDCS push, pregnancy/outcome, education, outreach calls, observation capture, CDA authoring, portal)
workflows.local_equivalent: healthstack-careplans-service (scaffold) + healthstack-patient + orders/observations + clinical-doc (CDA bridge) + healthstack-education (scaffold) + notify (outreach)
local_gaps: no pregnancy episode model (EDC, gestational age, baby records, outcomes)

suggested_representation.trigger: pregnancy confirmed -> care plan started; scheduled flag-eval and outreach windows
suggested_representation.permissions: coordinator/provider write; person owns own timeline; ConsentGuard; sensitive (IPV/depression) extra protection
ui: patient-app pregnancy timeline + education + self-tracking + appointment view
notifications: education + appointment + outreach to person; high-risk alert to coordinator

workflows.person_centric_reshape: Maternity-tracker is care-coordinator-worklist-centric with a pilot portal. Re-center on the pregnant person's journey: their own pregnancy timeline, milestones, education delivered at the right week, self-tracking, and gentle outreach they experience as support (not org call-tracking). The coordinator's high-risk flagging, outcome capture, CDA registry, and outreach-compliance are preserved as the safety + reporting layer riding on that journey.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native workflow-core (pregnancy-episode care plan) + build healthstack-careplans-service + clinical-doc CDA; scheduled flag-eval + outreach via automation-core (DSIO scheduled-task pattern); education via integrations-core

workflows.name: Family-History Capture
source_systems: FamilyHistoryCPRS (6 workflows: create patient entry, add relative, add disease, create FH note, load/display, edit)
workflows.local_equivalent: healthstack-problems-service (scaffold) + clinical-doc (FH note as Composition) + terminology (relationships/conditions) + builder-core (structured form)
local_gaps: no family-history data model (relatives, relationships, conditions w/ age-at-dx, ancestry)
no risk-relevant FH surfacing (ties to triage/CDS)

suggested_representation.trigger: person self-reports family history (patient-app) OR clinician captures during encounter
actors: person (best source of own family history)
suggested_representation.permissions: person edits own FH; clinician verify/annotate; ConsentGuard
ui: patient-app guided FH wizard (tree by relative)
jobs: FH note auto-generation on save (CreateFHNote analog)
integrations: terminology relationships/conditions/age-ranges (reference data via RPC analog)
notifications: FH-based screening recommendation to person
suggested_representation.audit: audit-core on FH edits; provider attribution

workflows.person_centric_reshape: FamilyHistoryCPRS is a clinician Delphi dialog with provider-DUZ on every RPC. Re-center: the person builds their own family tree at home (they know it best), choosing relatives and conditions in a friendly wizard; the clinician verifies and the system auto-generates the structured note and surfaces relevant screening recommendations to the person. The clinical-note + coded-pedigree requirements are preserved, sourced from the person instead of extracted by a clinician under time pressure.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native workflow-core (FH capture) + build FH model in healthstack-problems-service + builder-core form (reference-data-driven) + clinical-doc auto-note; FH-risk feeds hc.triage CDS

workflows.name: Procedural / Pre-procedure Checklist
source_systems: preproc-checklist (Assign, Complete, Close, Refresh items, Audit Logging)
workflows.local_equivalent: builder-core-service (form/schema builder, validation rules) + tasks-core (checklist items as tasks) + workflow-core (assign/complete/close lifecycle) + clinical-doc (resulting note)
local_gaps: no checklist template -> patient-instance assignment model
no logic engine for dependent-item evaluation (expressions, visibility)
no auto-refresh of items from live lab/vitals data

suggested_representation.trigger: procedure scheduled -> checklist assigned (template + procedure date)
suggested_representation.permissions: clinician/nurse mark items; person self-reports own prep items; ConsentGuard
jobs: refresh/re-evaluate logic from live labs/vitals (scheduled batch + on-demand)
notifications: prep reminders to person; blocked-item alert to clinician
suggested_representation.audit: audit-core full (user/IP/timestamp/change detail) - preproc FX_AUDIT analog

workflows.person_centric_reshape: preproc-checklist is clinician-form-centric (ASPX single-patient page). Re-center so the person owns their prep: they get a friendly pre-procedure checklist (fasting, meds-to-hold, transport) on their phone, self-report what they can, and the clinical/safety items auto-fill from live data. The org's logic-engine, dependent-item evaluation, refresh, and full audit are preserved as the clinical-safety completeness layer; the person experiences readiness, not a form.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native workflow-core (checklist lifecycle) + builder-core (template + logic/validation engine) + tasks-core items; auto-refresh from orders-service via automation-core; close emits clinical-doc note

workflows.name: Discharge (ADT)
workflows.local_equivalent: healthstack-patient-service (admission saga, in-memory FK pending M9) + healthstack-interop-service (scaffold, HL7v2 ADT TBD) + clinical-doc (discharge summary) + tasks-core (discharge tasks) + hc.avs
local_gaps: discharge saga (orders reconciliation, med reconciliation, follow-up scheduling, education) not fully modeled
no discharge-instruction person document beyond AVS

suggested_representation.trigger: provider initiates discharge
suggested_representation.permissions: provider order; nurse coordinate; ConsentGuard; ADT events tenant-scoped
ui: patient-app discharge instructions + meds + follow-up + warning signs
notifications: discharge instructions + follow-up to person; ADT to downstream systems

workflows.person_centric_reshape: ADT discharge in legacy systems is an inpatient state-flag + downstream messaging event. Re-center on the person leaving safely: a clear discharge plan (meds, follow-up already booked, warning signs, who to call) they carry home, with the org's med-reconciliation, order-resolution, ADT messaging, and readmission-prevention requirements satisfied as the gates that make that plan safe and complete.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native workflow-core (discharge saga) + healthstack-patient (move saga to Postgres) + clinical-doc summary + scheduling; build healthstack-interop-service for HL7v2 ADT; readmission follow-up via automation-core

workflows.name: Public-Health Reporting & Quality Measures
source_systems: pophealth (Measure Import, Quality Calculation map-reduce, Patient Import, Cohort Filter, QRDA/Excel Report)
workflows.local_equivalent: healthstack-quality-service (scaffold, HEDIS/CMS eCQM TBD) + reports-service (scheduled, Superset) + search-service (cohort) + terminology (value sets)
local_gaps: no measure definition (HQMF/eCQM) import + value-set loading

suggested_representation.trigger: measure imported; periodic calculation; reportable-condition detected (eCR)
suggested_representation.permissions: analyst/admin roles; de-identified/aggregate; ConsentGuard for any identified export; ABAC tenant
jobs: scheduled measure calculation (map-reduce over patients - pophealth analog)
notifications: calculation-complete to analyst; reportable-condition to public-health reporter

workflows.person_centric_reshape: pophealth and AWARE-QI are pure org/population analytics. Person-centric reshape: surface each person's own care gaps ('you're due for a colonoscopy / A1c') as actionable items in patient-app, derived from the same measure logic; the population-level eCQM/HEDIS calculation, QRDA submission, and QI compliance reporting are preserved as the aggregate roll-up of those per-person gaps. Public-health case reporting still meets mandates, but the person also benefits from gap-closure prompts.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native workflow-core (measure calc + reporting pipeline) + build healthstack-quality-service (map-reduce calc) + reports-service export + terminology value-sets; reportable-condition + agency submission via integrations-core/automation-core

workflows.name: Quote -> Order -> Invoice -> Payment (Order-to-Cash)
source_systems: erpnext (Sales Order, Sales Invoice, Payment Entry)
workflows.local_equivalent: sales-core-service (quotes/sales orders/commissions, partial) + commerce-core-service (Medusa v2 order state machine, real-working) + accounting-core-service (double-entry ledger, partial) + esign-core (quote sign-off)
local_gaps: accounting-core partial (GL posting on invoice/payment not fully wired)

suggested_representation.trigger: customer requests quote / accepts quote / order placed
suggested_representation.permissions: sales create quote; customer self-accept; accountant post GL; approver for discount thresholds; ABAC tenant
ui: customer self-serve quote-accept + order-track + pay portal
notifications: quote sent/accepted, invoice, payment receipt to customer (notify)
suggested_representation.audit: audit-core on every state + GL posting; tamper-evident

workflows.person_centric_reshape: Odoo/ERPNext order-to-cash is accountant/sales-rep-screen-centric with multi-step posting. Re-center on the buyer: self-serve quote acceptance, transparent order tracking, one-click pay, and clear receipts. The org's GL posting, tax computation, AR tracking, discount-approval, and bank reconciliation are preserved (no feature loss) but run behind the buyer-facing flow, and the multi-step accounting is simplified via automation/defaults as a CuraOS advantage.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native workflow-core (order-to-cash process) + sales-core + commerce-core (order state machine) + accounting-core ledger; payment reconciliation as automation-core + integrations-core bank-feed connector

workflows.name: Purchase Requisition -> PO -> Receipt -> Bill (Procure-to-Pay)
source_systems: erpnext (Purchase Order, Purchase Invoice, Stock Entry)
workflows.local_equivalent: procurement-core-service (vendor mgmt, RFQ, PO approval, 3-way match, partial) + inventory-core-service (stock, POs, partial) + accounting-core (AP)
local_gaps: 3-way match (PO/receipt/invoice) not wired to accounting AP posting

suggested_representation.trigger: requester creates purchase requisition
suggested_representation.permissions: requester create PR; approver thresholds; buyer issue PO; receiver confirm; AP post; ABAC tenant
notifications: PR approved/rejected to requester; PO to vendor; receipt confirmation; bill due

workflows.person_centric_reshape: Procure-to-pay in Odoo/ERPNext is buyer/AP-screen-centric. Re-center on the requester: an employee self-serves a simple 'I need X' request, sees real-time approval and delivery status, with the procurement rigor (RFQ, approval thresholds, 3-way match, AP posting) preserved behind it. The vendor also gets a clean self-serve portal. Org spend-control compliance is satisfied as a consequence of a clear requester journey.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native workflow-core (procure-to-pay + approval routing) + procurement-core + inventory-core + accounting-core AP; 3-way match as custom state machine within procurement-core; vendor portal via integrations-core

workflows.name: Inventory Reorder / Replenishment
workflows.local_equivalent: inventory-core-service (stock levels, warehouse, lot/expiration, partial) + procurement-core (auto-PO) + automation-core (scheduled reorder scan)
local_gaps: no reorder-point / min-max replenishment rule engine
no auto-PO generation on low stock

suggested_representation.trigger: scheduled stock scan OR stock movement drops below reorder point
suggested_representation.permissions: manager configure reorder rules; buyer approve auto-PO; ABAC by warehouse/tenant
notifications: low-stock + auto-PO-created to manager; stockout warning
suggested_representation.audit: audit-core on rule changes + auto-PO creation

workflows.person_centric_reshape: Inventory replenishment is inherently org-operational, but re-center the person who depends on stock: in healthstack the 'person' is the patient/clinician who must never face a stockout of a needed medication/supply, so reorder rules prioritize patient-critical and expiration-sensitive items; the inventory manager's min/max/MRP machinery is preserved. For business, the storefront customer never sees out-of-stock for fast movers.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native automation-core (scheduled reorder scan + conditional branching) + inventory-core reorder rules + procurement-core auto-PO; MRP as custom logic in inventory/manufacturing; cron via automation-core (ir_cron pattern)

workflows.name: Contract Create / Renewal
workflows.local_equivalent: esign-core-service (multi-party envelopes, eIDAS/UETA, real-working) + documents-core + workflow-core (contract lifecycle) + notify (renewal alerts) + recurrence package (scaffold)

suggested_representation.trigger: contract drafted; renewal window approaches (scheduled)
suggested_representation.permissions: AM draft; customer sign (esign); legal review gate; ABAC tenant
notifications: sign request, executed, renewal-due to customer + AM

workflows.person_centric_reshape: Contract/renewal in ERP is account-manager-document-centric. Re-center on the customer: they sign in one tap, always have a clear copy, see renewal terms ahead of time with no surprise auto-renewal, and can self-serve renew/amend. The org's signature legality (eIDAS/UETA), versioning, approval, and renewal-revenue tracking are preserved as the trust + revenue layer behind a transparent customer experience.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native workflow-core (contract lifecycle) + esign-core + documents-core; renewal scheduling via recurrence package + automation-core (auto_repeat pattern)

workflows.name: Subscription Billing
workflows.local_equivalent: commerce-core (Medusa, Stripe/PayPal) + accounting-core (recurring invoice) + recurrence package (scaffold) + automation-core (scheduled generation) + donation-core (recurring donation analog)
local_gaps: no scheduled invoice generation from subscription

suggested_representation.trigger: billing-schedule cycle date reached (cron)
suggested_representation.permissions: customer manage own subscription (pause/cancel); admin override; ABAC tenant
notifications: upcoming charge, receipt, failed-payment, dunning to subscriber

workflows.person_centric_reshape: Subscription billing in ERP is a scheduler producing invoices behind the scenes. Re-center on the subscriber: full self-serve control (pause, upgrade, cancel anytime), clear upcoming-charge notice, and graceful failed-payment handling that informs rather than silently churns. The org's recurring revenue, proration, GL posting, and dunning are preserved; involuntary churn is reduced as a CuraOS advantage.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native automation-core (scheduled invoice generation + retry/dunning ladder) + recurrence package + accounting-core + commerce payment; cron via automation-core (auto_repeat/ir_cron pattern)

workflows.name: Expense Approval
workflows.local_equivalent: hr-core-service (expenses, partial) + workflow-core-service (human tasks, BPMN approval) + business-automation-service (approval gate) + notify
local_gaps: hr-core partial (expense submission/approval not fully built)
approval-routing rules (amount thresholds, AssignmentRule analog) not configured

suggested_representation.trigger: employee submits expense claim
suggested_representation.permissions: employee submit own; manager approve direct reports; finance threshold; ABAC tenant; segregation of duties
notifications: approval request to manager; approved/rejected/reimbursed to employee

workflows.person_centric_reshape: Expense approval in ERP is an org approval-chain doctype. Re-center on the employee: snap a receipt on mobile, submit in seconds, see exactly where the approval is and when reimbursement lands. The org's policy enforcement, threshold routing, segregation of duties, and accounting posting are preserved as the control layer behind a fast, transparent employee experience.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native workflow-core (human-approval BPMN) + hr-core expense + accounting-core; routing rules as automation-core (AssignmentRule/Frappe-workflow pattern); approval pause/resume mirrors windmill/activepieces approval loop

workflows.name: Project / Task / Timesheet Billing
workflows.local_equivalent: business-projects-service (scaffold-only) + tasks-core-service (lifecycle, assignment, dependencies, real-working) + accounting-core (invoice) + hr-core (time)

suggested_representation.trigger: project created -> tasks assigned -> time logged
suggested_representation.permissions: member log own time; PM approve; accountant invoice; client view; ABAC tenant
notifications: time-approval, milestone, invoice to relevant person

workflows.person_centric_reshape: Project billing in ERP is PM/accountant-grid-centric. Re-center two persons: the team member logs time effortlessly from where they work (calendar-integrated, mobile) and sees their own contribution; the client sees a transparent project portal with progress and clear invoices. The org's WIP, billable-rate, approval, and revenue-recognition rigor are preserved behind both person surfaces.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native workflow-core (project->billing) + build business-projects-service + tasks-core + hr-core time + accounting-core; finish business-projects-service from scaffold

workflows.name: Sales Pipeline (Lead -> Opportunity -> Close)
workflows.local_equivalent: crm-core-service (contacts/companies, opportunity pipeline, activities, partial) + business-automation (recipes) + notify
local_gaps: crm-core partial (pipeline stage automation, probability, auto-assign incomplete)

suggested_representation.trigger: lead captured (form/import/webhook)
suggested_representation.permissions: rep owns assigned leads; manager all; ABAC tenant; consent for marketing contact
notifications: new-lead to rep; nurture to prospect (consent-gated); stage updates
suggested_representation.audit: audit-core on assignment/stage changes; marketing-consent record

workflows.person_centric_reshape: CRM pipelines are pure org/sales-rep funnels. Re-center on the prospect as a person: respect their consent and preferences, give them a genuinely helpful buying journey (clear info, easy proposal acceptance, no spam), so trust drives conversion. The org's pipeline stages, forecasting, auto-assignment, and campaign machinery are preserved but oriented to serving the prospect well, which improves close rates honestly.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native crm-core pipeline + business-automation/automation-core for assignment + nurture (SuiteCRM AOW_WorkFlow / Odoo CRM pattern); campaigns via notify + integrations-core

workflows.name: Customer Onboarding
workflows.local_equivalent: crm-core + workflow-core (onboarding process) + tasks-core (onboarding checklist) + esign-core (agreements) + identity-service (account provisioning) + notify
local_gaps: no onboarding orchestration (account setup, agreement sign, kickoff, provisioning)

suggested_representation.trigger: deal won / new customer created
suggested_representation.permissions: specialist drive; customer self-complete steps; ABAC tenant
notifications: welcome + step prompts + completion to customer

workflows.person_centric_reshape: Onboarding is often an internal specialist checklist. Re-center on the customer: a guided self-serve onboarding wizard they drive at their pace, with the specialist as a helper, automated welcome sequence, and clear 'you're all set' moment. The org's provisioning, agreement-signing, and setup-completeness requirements are satisfied as the customer completes their own journey.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native workflow-core (onboarding process) + tasks-core checklist + esign-core + identity/tenancy provisioning; welcome automation via automation-core

workflows.name: Lead Intake
workflows.local_equivalent: crm-core-service (contacts, partial) + builder-core (intake form) + integrations-core (webhook inbound) + business-automation (logic-hook analog)
local_gaps: no insurance-specific lead intake (coverage interest, current carrier)
no logic-hook style on-save automation (field updates, record creation)
no multi-channel intake (web form, webhook, email)

suggested_representation.trigger: prospect submits intake (web form / webhook / email)
suggested_representation.permissions: agent owns assigned; coordinator route; consent for contact; ABAC tenant
integrations: web form, webhook, email channels via integrations-core
notifications: new-lead to agent; acknowledgement to prospect

workflows.person_centric_reshape: SuiteCRM lead intake is a record + logic-hook on save. Re-center on the prospect: a simple, respectful intake that asks only what's needed, confirms receipt immediately, and connects them to a real broker fast (SLA on first contact). The org's dedup, enrichment, assignment, and logic-hook automation are preserved as the behind-the-scenes machinery that makes the prospect feel promptly served.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native crm-core + builder-core intake form + automation-core (SuiteCRM AOW_WorkFlow/logic-hook pattern); inbound channels via integrations-core webhook

workflows.name: Broker / Customer Onboarding (Insurance)
workflows.local_equivalent: crm-core (accounts/contacts) + esign-core (producer agreements) + identity-service (broker account) + workflow-core (KYC/appointment process) + documents-core

suggested_representation.trigger: new broker recruited / new insured customer onboarded
suggested_representation.permissions: compliance gate; broker self-complete application; ABAC tenant
notifications: application status, missing docs, activation to broker

workflows.person_centric_reshape: Broker onboarding in CRM is a compliance-officer document chase. Re-center on the broker: a clear self-serve application showing exactly what's needed and where they are, with the compliance/KYC/license/appointment rigor preserved as gates the system manages for them. The insured customer's onboarding similarly becomes a guided self-serve flow rather than an agent-entered record.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native workflow-core (KYC/onboarding gates) + crm-core (broker hierarchy model) + esign-core + identity provisioning; carrier appointment + license verification via integrations-core

workflows.name: Insurance Quote Request
workflows.local_equivalent: sales-core (quotes, partial) + crm-core + builder-core (rating intake form) + workflow-core (quote process)
local_gaps: no insurance rating/quote engine (coverage lines, premium calc)

suggested_representation.trigger: customer requests insurance quote
suggested_representation.permissions: agent quote; customer self-request/accept; underwriter referral; ABAC tenant
ui: customer self-serve quote request + compare + accept
notifications: quote ready, expiring, accepted to customer

workflows.person_centric_reshape: Insurance quoting is a broker workbench task. Re-center on the customer: a simple self-serve request, transparent multi-carrier comparison in plain terms, and one-click acceptance to bind, with the broker available to advise. The org's rating rigor, underwriter referral, and carrier integration are preserved behind a transparent comparison the customer controls.
workflows.implementation_mode: custom state machine for rating/quote + local native workflow-core orchestration + sales-core quote + builder-core rating intake; carrier rating via integrations-core; binds to crm.policy

workflows.name: Policy Create / Renewal
source_files: suitecrm policy module + AOR scheduled reports (renewal alerts)
workflows.local_equivalent: workflow-core (policy lifecycle) + esign-core (policy docs) + accounting-core (premium billing) + recurrence (renewal schedule, scaffold) + notify (renewal alerts)
local_gaps: no policy entity (terms, coverage lines, premium schedule, installments, beneficiaries)
no renewal automation + premium installment billing

suggested_representation.trigger: quote accepted -> bind policy; renewal window approaches
suggested_representation.permissions: broker manage; policyholder view/self-renew; ABAC tenant; beneficiary consent
ui: policyholder portal (policy docs, coverage, pay installment, renew)
notifications: policy issued, installment due, renewal due, lapse warning to policyholder

workflows.person_centric_reshape: Policy administration in CRM is broker-book-of-business-centric. Re-center on the policyholder: a clear portal showing exactly what they're covered for, easy installment payment, advance renewal terms with no nasty surprises, and self-serve renewal. The org's policy-term, premium-schedule, beneficiary, carrier-sync, and renewal-revenue machinery is fully preserved as the engine behind a transparent policyholder experience.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native workflow-core (policy lifecycle) + esign-core + accounting-core (premium installments) + recurrence (renewal scheduling, auto_repeat pattern); carrier sync via integrations-core

workflows.name: Policy Endorsement (Mid-term Change)
workflows.local_equivalent: workflow-core (endorsement state machine) + accounting-core (premium adjustment/proration) + esign-core (endorsement doc) + crm.policy

suggested_representation.trigger: policyholder requests mid-term change (add coverage, change limits, etc.)
suggested_representation.permissions: policyholder request; broker process; underwriter referral threshold; ABAC tenant
notifications: endorsement status + premium change to policyholder

workflows.person_centric_reshape: Endorsements are broker-processed policy edits. Re-center on the policyholder: they request a coverage change in plain language, instantly see the premium impact (proration), sign, and it's applied, with the org's underwriter-referral, rating, and carrier-sync requirements preserved as the controls that make the change valid and accurate.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native workflow-core (endorsement state machine, frappe-workflow pattern) + accounting-core proration + esign-core; carrier endorsement via integrations-core; extends crm.policy

workflows.name: Insurance Claim Intake & Follow-up
workflows.local_equivalent: business-cases-service (scaffold-only) + workflow-core (claim lifecycle) + documents-core (claim docs) + tasks-core (follow-up) + notify
local_gaps: no claim entity (FNOL, status, adjuster, settlement)

suggested_representation.trigger: policyholder files first notice of loss (FNOL)
suggested_representation.permissions: claimant file/track own; adjuster process; ABAC tenant; consent for shared data
ui: claimant self-serve FNOL + document upload + status tracker
notifications: claim received, docs needed, status updates, settlement to claimant

workflows.person_centric_reshape: Insurance claims in CRM are adjuster/case-management-centric, and claimants are anxious and in the dark. Re-center entirely on the claimant: easy FNOL from their phone, clear list of needed documents, real-time status, and fast settlement. The org's SLA, adjuster routing, document-collection, and carrier integration are preserved as the engine ensuring no claim stalls. This is the highest-empathy reshape: a person in a loss event gets clarity, not a black box.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native workflow-core (claim lifecycle) + build business-cases-service + documents-core + tasks-core; SLA/follow-up via automation-core (erpnext SLA-escalation pattern); carrier via integrations-core

workflows.name: Commission Calculation & Approval
workflows.local_equivalent: sales-core-service (commissions, partial) + accounting-core (commission payable) + workflow-core (approval) + crm-core (broker hierarchy)

suggested_representation.trigger: policy bound/premium paid -> commission event; periodic commission run
suggested_representation.permissions: manager approve; broker view own statement; ABAC tenant; SoD
notifications: statement ready, approved, paid to broker
suggested_representation.audit: audit-core full commission + approval trail; SoD

workflows.person_centric_reshape: Commission processing is a finance back-office run. Re-center on the broker as a person who earns a living: a transparent, real-time commission statement they can trust, clear splits/overrides, and predictable payment. The org's multi-level hierarchy, split rules, approval, clawback, and carrier-statement reconciliation are preserved as the accuracy layer behind a statement the broker actually understands.
workflows.implementation_mode: custom state machine for commission calc (splits/overrides) + local native workflow-core approval + sales-core + accounting-core payable; scheduled run via automation-core; carrier statements via integrations-core

workflows.name: Support Case / Service Ticket
workflows.local_equivalent: business-cases-service (scaffold-only) + workflow-core (case lifecycle) + notify (SLA notifications) + tasks-core

suggested_representation.trigger: customer opens support case (portal/email/webhook)
suggested_representation.permissions: agent owns assigned; manager escalation; customer track own; ABAC tenant
notifications: case received, agent reply, resolution to customer; SLA breach to manager
suggested_representation.audit: audit-core on case lifecycle + SLA events

workflows.person_centric_reshape: Support ticketing is an agent-queue + SLA-metric system. Re-center on the customer in trouble: a clear case portal showing real progress, proactive updates, and fast resolution, with the org's SLA tracking, escalation, and auto-close serving as the guarantee the customer is never forgotten rather than internal-only metrics.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native workflow-core (case lifecycle) + build business-cases-service + notify; SLA timers + breach escalation via automation-core (erpnext SLA-escalation pattern); channels via integrations-core

workflows.name: Document Collection (KYC / Underwriting)
workflows.local_equivalent: documents-core-service (metadata, versioning, retention) + storage-service (blobs, virus scan) + builder-core (upload form) + workflow-core (collection lifecycle) + business-docs-service (scaffold)
local_gaps: no document-collection workflow (request list, track received, validate, complete)

suggested_representation.trigger: process requires documents (onboarding/claim/policy/KYC)
suggested_representation.permissions: person upload own; reviewer validate; ABAC tenant; PII protection
ui: person self-serve upload portal (checklist with status)
notifications: documents needed, received, validated, rejected to person

workflows.person_centric_reshape: Document collection in CRM is a compliance-officer chase via email. Re-center on the person: a single clear checklist showing exactly what's needed and what's received, drag-drop upload from any device, and instant validation feedback. The org's KYC/underwriting completeness, validation, virus-scanning, and retention requirements are preserved as the controls behind a frictionless upload experience.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native workflow-core (collection lifecycle) + documents-core + storage-service + builder-core form (activepieces/n8n form-trigger pattern); finish business-docs-service; nudges via automation-core

workflows.name: Compliance Review
workflows.local_equivalent: workflow-core (review state machine) + audit-core-service (SHA-256 hash-chain, GDPR disclosure) + policy package (scaffold) + tasks-core
local_gaps: no compliance-review workflow (flag, review, attest, remediate)

suggested_representation.trigger: compliance event/flag, periodic review schedule, or risk threshold
suggested_representation.permissions: compliance roles; segregation of duties; ABAC tenant; tamper-evident
notifications: flag, remediation-due, attestation-needed to relevant role
suggested_representation.audit: audit-core immutable hash-chain (the core artifact); GDPR Art.15 disclosure

workflows.person_centric_reshape: Compliance review is inherently org/regulator-facing, but re-center on the persons it protects: customers and brokers whose data and rights are at stake. Surface to each person their own data-rights status (consent, disclosure requests, GDPR Art.15) via patient/customer portal, while the org's full compliance-review, remediation, attestation, and immutable-audit machinery runs to satisfy regulators. The person experiences trustworthy data stewardship.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native workflow-core (review state machine, frappe-workflow pattern) + audit-core (immutable trail) + policy package (rule engine, finish from scaffold) + tasks-core; periodic scans via automation-core

workflows.name: Human Approval Step
workflows.local_equivalent: workflow-core-service (BPMN human tasks, real-working) + business-automation-service (approval gate, real-working) + notify-service
local_gaps: approval step in automation-core not surfaced as reusable primitive across all workflows
no multi-approver / parallel / threshold approval config

suggested_representation.trigger: workflow/automation reaches an approval step
suggested_representation.permissions: approver role per step (allowed_role, frappe pattern); ABAC tenant; SoD
ui: approver inbox + one-click approve/reject (with resume link, windmill/activepieces pattern)
notifications: approval request + reminders to approver; outcome to requester

workflows.person_centric_reshape: Approval steps are org-control primitives. Person-centric reshape: make approval frictionless for the approver-as-person (one-tap mobile approve with full context, no hunting), and make the requester-as-person never wait in the dark (live status, ETA). The org's control/SoD/threshold requirements are preserved while both human participants get a respectful experience.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native workflow-core human-task + automation-core approval gate as a reusable primitive (windmill Approval Decision Loop / activepieces pause-resume pattern); notify for approval links; resume via integrations-core webhook

workflows.name: Scheduled Jobs (Cron)
workflows.local_equivalent: automation-core-service (scheduling, real-working) + reports-service (scheduled jobs) + workflow-core (timer events)
local_gaps: no timezone-aware cron with handler chains (windmill schedule pattern: skip-condition + success/failure/recovery handlers)
no central scheduled-job registry/observability across services
each service implements own cron ad hoc

suggested_representation.trigger: cron expression (timezone-aware) reached
suggested_representation.permissions: admin configure; ABAC tenant; ops read-only monitor
integrations: any downstream service via automation-core actions
notifications: job-failure to ops; digest to admin
suggested_representation.audit: audit-core on schedule changes + run outcomes

workflows.person_centric_reshape: Scheduled jobs are pure machine automation, but they exist to serve persons: reminders, renewals, recalls, statements. Reshape so every scheduled job is framed by the person-outcome it produces (e.g., 'send the person their appointment reminder' not 'run reminder cron'), and surface to admins which person-facing outcomes each schedule guarantees, so silent failures are caught as person-impact.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native automation-core (timezone-aware cron + handler chains, windmill schedule.rs pattern) as the single scheduling primitive; workflow-core timer events for in-process timers; reports-service for scheduled exports

workflows.name: Webhook / Event-driven Sync
source_systems: windmill (Event Trigger to Job, Job Queue Lifecycle)
activepieces (Webhook Inbound, Polling Trigger, Trigger Flow Execution)
n8n (Webhook Trigger, HTTP Request, Respond to Webhook)
workflows.local_equivalent: integrations-core-service (connector framework, OAuth2/API-key vault, webhooks, retry/backoff, real-working) + event-core-service (Kafka topic/schema registry) + automation-core (event-driven actions)
local_gaps: no inbound-webhook-to-workflow trigger primitive in automation-core (activepieces/n8n pattern)
outbound webhook (frappe pattern) exists in integrations-core but not exposed per-workflow
no multi-source event trigger (Kafka/Postgres-LISTEN/MQTT, windmill native-triggers pattern)

suggested_representation.trigger: inbound webhook POST / external event (Kafka/Postgres/MQTT) / polling interval
suggested_representation.permissions: admin configure connector/webhook; signature verification; ABAC tenant
integrations: any external SaaS/system (OAuth2/API-key/HMAC, frappe security pattern)
notifications: connector-failure to admin
suggested_representation.audit: audit-core on connector config + event processing; payload reference-only (PHI boundary)

workflows.person_centric_reshape: Event sync is machine plumbing, but its purpose is keeping a person's data consistent everywhere (their record, their order, their policy). Reshape so each integration is documented by the person-data it keeps in sync and respects the PHI/PII boundary (reference-only events, consent-gated), so the person's data flows correctly without ever being over-exposed across systems.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native integrations-core (connector framework + inbound/outbound webhooks + retry/backoff, frappe/activepieces pattern) + event-core (multi-source triggers, windmill native-triggers pattern) + automation-core event-driven actions; OpenELIS-style feeds as source background service connector

workflows.name: AI Summarization / Extraction / Workflow-Generation
source_systems: activepieces (AI Agent Tool Execution, MCP Tool Execution)
n8n (AI Transform Node, Chat Hub Session)
workflows.local_equivalent: automation-core (action catalog, can host AI actions) + plugin-runtime-service (WASM/Node sandbox for AI tools) + conversion-core (PDF/FHIR/HL7/CSV extraction, partial)
local_gaps: no AI action/step primitive (LLM summarize/extract/transform) in automation-core
no AI-agent tool-execution (MCP tooling) in automation-core/plugin-runtime
must honor PHI boundary + agent-eval/observability rule (DeepEval+Langfuse+Presidio)

suggested_representation.trigger: workflow reaches AI step / chat input / document to extract
suggested_representation.permissions: admin configure model/tools; PHI requires consent + redaction; ABAC tenant; eval-gated
apis: plugin-runtime sandbox (tool exec, MCP, activepieces pattern)
integrations: LLM providers via @curaos/providers (local-first, no SaaS lock-in)
notifications: low-confidence -> human review; eval-threshold breach to admin
suggested_representation.audit: audit-core on AI invocations; eval scores logged; redaction enforced; no PHI to external LLM without consent

workflows.person_centric_reshape: AI steps in n8n/activepieces are generic transforms. Person-centric reshape: use AI to reduce the person's burden (auto-summarize their visit, extract data from their uploaded document, draft their AVS) and the clinician's/agent's burden, always with PHI redaction, consent, human-review for anything clinical or low-confidence, and full eval/observability. AI serves the person's quality-of-life and never the org at the expense of the person's data safety.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native automation-core AI action + plugin-runtime sandbox (activepieces MCP/agent pattern) + conversion-core extraction; LLM via @curaos/providers (local-first); Presidio redaction + DeepEval/Langfuse per curaos-agent-eval-obs-rule; PHI boundary enforced

workflows.name: Retry / Error Handling
workflows.local_equivalent: automation-core (run history, conditional branching) + integrations-core (retry/backoff) + event-core (DLQ/outbox) + workflow-core (incidents)
local_gaps: no unified retry policy primitive (backoff, max-attempts, from-failed-step, activepieces pattern)
no error-handler/catch primitive (node-red catch / n8n Error Trigger pattern)

suggested_representation.trigger: step/job/flow error
suggested_representation.permissions: ops manual-retry; admin configure retry policy; ABAC tenant
notifications: persistent-failure/incident to ops

workflows.person_centric_reshape: Error handling is invisible machinery, but its failures hurt persons (a lost reminder, a stuck payment, an unsent claim). Reshape so error policies are tied to person-impact severity: failures affecting a person's care/money/safety escalate fast and surface a clear 'we hit a snag, here's what's happening' to the person rather than a silent dead-letter. Idempotency protects the person from double-effects (double-charge, double-order).
workflows.implementation_mode: local native automation-core (retry policy + error-handler primitive, activepieces/windmill/node-red pattern) + integrations-core backoff + event-core DLQ + workflow-core incidents; compensation/rollback as saga in workflow-core

workflows.name: Escalation
workflows.local_equivalent: workflow-core (timer/escalation events) + automation-core (conditional + scheduled) + notify (multi-channel) + tasks-core
local_gaps: no reusable escalation-ladder primitive (L1->L2->supervisor by elapsed time, aware/erpnext pattern)

suggested_representation.trigger: SLA/time threshold breached, no acknowledgement, or condition unmet
suggested_representation.permissions: escalation targets per ladder config; ABAC tenant
notifications: each escalation rung notifies next target; supervisor on top rung

workflows.person_centric_reshape: Escalation exists to ensure a person is served when the primary path fails (a critical result acted on, a claim not stalled, a ticket resolved). Reshape escalation ladders to be measured by person-outcome (the person got helped) not org-process-completion, and inform the affected person that their issue is being actively escalated, never leaving them wondering.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native workflow-core (escalation-ladder timer primitive, aware/erpnext SLA pattern) + automation-core scheduling + notify + tasks-core reassign; reusable across hc.critical_alert, crm.support_case, erp.expense_approval

workflows.name: Audit Review
workflows.local_equivalent: audit-core-service (SHA-256 hash-chain store, Kafka audit consumer, GDPR Art.15 disclosure, real-working) + reports-service (audit reports) + search-service
local_gaps: no audit-review workflow (anomaly detection, periodic review, access-review attestation)

suggested_representation.trigger: periodic audit-review schedule, anomaly/break-glass event, or compliance request
suggested_representation.permissions: auditor read-all (immutable); DPO for PHI access review; SoD; ABAC tenant
notifications: break-glass event to DPO; anomaly to auditor; attestation-due to management
suggested_representation.audit: the audit trail itself is the artifact; review actions are themselves audited (immutable, tamper-evident)

workflows.person_centric_reshape: Audit review is org/regulator-facing, but it protects each person's data rights. Reshape to give each person visibility into who accessed their record and why (access-disclosure in patient/customer portal, GDPR Art.15 self-serve), turning the audit trail into a transparency feature for the person while the org's compliance review, break-glass oversight, and anomaly detection run on the same immutable trail.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native audit-core (immutable hash-chain, the system of record) + reports-service for review reports + search-service; periodic access-review + anomaly scan via automation-core; break-glass review as workflow-core human task

workflows.name: Admin Override / Break-glass
workflows.local_equivalent: identity-service (break-glass logged w/ reason, RBAC reload) + workflow-core (override approval) + audit-core (break-glass record) + healthstack-consent (consent override logging)
local_gaps: no reusable admin-override primitive (force-state, bypass-gate) with mandatory reason + approval + audit
break-glass exists in identity but not surfaced as a workflow step for clinical/business gates

suggested_representation.trigger: admin/clinician invokes override (force state, bypass approval, emergency access)
suggested_representation.permissions: privileged role + mandatory reason; emergency break-glass logged immediately; post-hoc review required; ABAC tenant
jobs: post-hoc review timer (must review every override)
notifications: break-glass to DPO immediately; post-hoc review-due to supervisor
suggested_representation.audit: audit-core mandatory tamper-evident record with reason + actor + affected entity; the defining requirement

workflows.person_centric_reshape: Admin override/break-glass is pure org-privilege, but it touches a person's protected data at the most sensitive moment (emergency care). Reshape so the person is protected and informed: every break-glass access to their record is logged with reason, surfaced to them via access-disclosure, and post-hoc reviewed. The org's emergency-access capability (life-safety) is fully preserved (no feature loss) but wrapped in mandatory reason + audit + person-disclosure so trust is never sacrificed for power.
workflows.implementation_mode: local native identity-service break-glass + workflow-core override step (mandatory-reason + optional approval) + audit-core (immutable record) + healthstack-consent override logging; post-hoc review via automation-core scheduled + workflow-core human task

runtime_strategy.summary: CuraOS already owns the workflow/automation/builder substrate locally; the external engines (Windmill, Activepieces, Node-RED, n8n) are reference patterns to MINE for completeness, NOT to install. None are present in the local inventory. Per the builder+BPM-first charter and generator-first law, every mapped workflow lands as configuration on the local native substrate, with shared primitives extended into the generator/SDK, never per-app hand-edits.

local_substrate.workflow-core-service: BPMN 2.0 (Zeebe/Camunda), process instances, human tasks, incidents - PRIMARY engine for stateful clinical/business processes (encounter, discharge saga, referral closed-loop, policy/claim lifecycle, escalation ladders).
local_substrate.automation-core-service: low-code actions/connectors, scheduling, conditional branching, run history - PRIMARY for cron/scheduled jobs, event-driven actions, retry/error policy, AI steps, reminders/recall/dunning.
local_substrate.integrations-core-service: connector framework, OAuth2/API-key vault, webhooks, retry/backoff - PRIMARY for inbound/outbound webhook + event-sync + external SaaS/carrier/payer/PACS/LIS connectors and source background-service feeds (OpenELIS atom-feed pattern).
local_substrate.event-core-service: Kafka/Redpanda topic+schema registry, contract versioning, transactional outbox - event-led default for cross-service interaction, DLQ, multi-source triggers.
local_substrate.builder-core-service + builder-studio: form/schema builder + logic/validation engine + GrapesJS no-code surfaces + rjsf forms - intake forms, checklists (preproc logic engine), AVS section config, document-collection checklists.
local_substrate.workflow-designer: @xyflow/react canvas (node graph) - the visual authoring surface for automation flows.
local_substrate.plugin-runtime-service: WASM/Node sandbox - hosts AI agent tool execution + MCP tools (activepieces agent/MCP pattern) safely.
local_substrate.audit-core-service: SHA-256 hash-chain + GDPR Art.15 - the immutable system of record for every audit/compliance/break-glass workflow.
local_substrate.supporting: recurrence (renewal/billing schedules), policy (compliance rule engine), forms, canvas packages are SCAFFOLD-ONLY and must be finished to back several workflows.

local native workflow-core (BPMN): stateful lifecycles: registration, appointment/check-in, encounter, referral closed-loop, discharge saga, contract, policy, endorsement, claim intake, onboarding, customer onboarding, support case, compliance review, human approval, escalation ladders
local native automation-core (cron/event: scheduled jobs, webhook/event sync, AI summarize/extract, retry/error, reminders/recall/dunning, inventory reorder, subscription billing generation, escalation timers
custom state machine (domain calc engine: triage/risk scoring (asrcm pattern), radiology protocol/contraindication (RAPTOR ContraIndEngine), insurance rating/quote, commission split/override calc
source background service pattern (feeds: LIS/OpenELIS atom-feed ingest, ADT/HL7v2 interop, audit ETL (aware SQL Transporter analog), CDA registry push
reference-only (NOT installed): Windmill schedule/approval/queue patterns, Activepieces pause-resume/agent/MCP/polling patterns, Node-RED message/catch/flow-deploy patterns, n8n trigger/wait/error-trigger patterns - these inform the design of local primitives only

runtime_strategy.generator_first_directive: New workflow capabilities enter via the mold: TypeSpec/AsyncAPI contracts -> @curaos/*-sdk regen -> emitServiceLive scaffold -> emitUiApp FE wiring. Reusable workflow primitives (human-approval step, escalation ladder, retry policy, scheduled-job-with-handler-chain, webhook trigger, AI step) must be added to @curaos/codegen templates/emitters and the workflow/automation SDKs so every service inherits them, NOT hand-coded per service (curaos-generator-evolution-rule).
data_truth_blockers: healthstack-patient FK, healthstack-billing store, healthstack-messaging thread store are IN-MEMORY (pending M9 Drizzle) - workflows depending on them (registration, discharge, claim/billing) are not data-truth-compliant until moved to Postgres seeds/fixtures (curaos-demo-sample-data-rule).
scaffold_gaps_to_finish: business-cases-service (claim intake, support case), business-projects-service (project billing), business-scheduling-service (appointment), business-docs-service + business-conversion-service (document collection), healthstack-lab/meds/imaging/careplans/claims/quality/notes/problems/ems/devices/interop/education-service (clinical workflows), recurrence/policy/forms/canvas/plugin-runtime packages.
runtime_strategy.person_centric_dominant_pattern: Per the LENS, every workflow yields DUAL surfaces on shared data+contract: a person-facing journey surface (patient-app / customer portal / broker self-serve: simple, journey-driven) AND a management surface (clinician/admin/business: full compliance + control). The org process is satisfied as a consequence of serving the person well; no source business/management/compliance feature is dropped, only re-sequenced, defaulted, and automated.
